summary: Accessed on 2026-04-23. External sources were used only to keep the Phase 5 exit interpretation aligned with current Playwright evidence packaging, NHS cross-organisation identity and messaging context, and current accessibility expectations. The repository blueprints and validated Phase 5 outputs remained authoritative for product law, verdict thresholds, and carry-forward boundaries.

sources.title: Trace viewer | Playwright
borrowedInto: The 341 evidence guide and exit board both treat trace artifacts as first-class audit links for skeptical release review.
rejectedOrConstrained: Trace availability was not treated as sufficient release proof by itself; verdicts still depend on typed capability rows and machine-readable evidence bundles.

sources.title: Reporters | Playwright
borrowedInto: The 341 gate pack keeps human-readable and machine-readable evidence aligned rather than relying on a raw runner dump.
rejectedOrConstrained: The generic Playwright HTML report was not substituted for the repo-owned release gate board, because the board must express contract posture and carry-forward law.

sources.title: Command line | Playwright
borrowedInto: The evidence review guide explicitly keeps trace-opening and local replay commands simple and auditable.
rejectedOrConstrained: CLI convenience was not allowed to replace the repo's own validator and board proof.

sources.title: Care Identity Service (CIS) - NHS England Digital
borrowedInto: The exit interpretation keeps cross-organisation authority bounded to governed identity and access management rather than UI convention.
rejectedOrConstrained: CIS product framing did not override the local ActingContext and ActingScopeTuple contract vocabulary.

sources.title: Role selection - NHS England Digital
borrowedInto: The gate keeps organisation and role switching as explicit release-review concerns for stale-scope and read-only recovery proof.
rejectedOrConstrained: NHS role-selection mechanics were not imported as a substitute for the local break-glass, supersession, and frozen-posture law.

sources.title: Care Identity Management - NHS England Digital
borrowedInto: The carry-forward interpretation keeps access assignment and authenticator governance outside the repo-owned browser proofs.
rejectedOrConstrained: Care Identity Management operations were not mistaken for evidence that the repo has completed live access onboarding.

sources.title: Message Exchange for Social Care and Health - NHS England Digital
borrowedInto: The gate keeps MESH continuity as a nationally recognized messaging boundary and treats repo automation as local-twin plus manual bridge where lawful access is absent.
rejectedOrConstrained: National MESH service framing did not allow the repo to overclaim that path-to-live mailbox setup is fully automated.

sources.title: Message Exchange for Social Care and Health (MESH): Workflow Groups and Workflow IDs - NHS England Digital
borrowedInto: The exit gate preserves workflow-ID mapping as a live operational boundary rather than flattening it into local route proof.
rejectedOrConstrained: Workflow definitions did not override the repo-owned message truth and acknowledgement debt semantics.

sources.title: Understanding Success Criterion 4.1.3: Status Messages | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: 341 treats browser-visible status truth as release-critical, not optional polish.
rejectedOrConstrained: WCAG status-message guidance informed the gate threshold only; the actual status vocabulary still comes from the local truth model.

sources.title: Understanding Success Criterion 1.4.10: Reflow | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: The exit gate keeps the 340 narrow-screen and high-zoom proofs as release evidence rather than secondary UX notes.
rejectedOrConstrained: Reflow guidance did not soften the requirement that selected anchors and dominant actions remain stable.

sources.title: Understanding Success Criterion 2.4.13: Focus Appearance | WAI | W3C
borrowedInto: Focus order, visible focus, and focus return remain gate-worthy proof expectations for the hub and patient surfaces.
rejectedOrConstrained: The criterion was not used to weaken the local requirement for truthful bounded recovery under scope drift.

sources.title: Confirmation page – NHS digital service manual
borrowedInto: The exit interpretation keeps patient-facing confirmation calm and concise only where the underlying truth is actually confirmed.
rejectedOrConstrained: NHS confirmation-page guidance was not allowed to overrule the repo's pending-versus-confirmed truth boundary.

sources.title: Interruption page – NHS digital service manual
borrowedInto: The gate keeps denied, stale-scope, and recovery postures explicit rather than interpreting silent redirect as acceptable.
rejectedOrConstrained: Interruption-page guidance did not justify adding harsher blocking flows than the local same-shell recovery law requires.

sources.title: Triage – Linear Docs
borrowedInto: The gate board uses triage-friendly grouping so blockers, carry-forward, and proof rows scan quickly.
rejectedOrConstrained: Linear workflow patterns were not adopted as release semantics.

sources.title: Vercel Design
borrowedInto: The board keeps a restrained, high-signal presentation instead of a noisy dashboard aesthetic.
rejectedOrConstrained: Vercel visual style did not override the repo's required palette or release-control structure.

sources.title: Data table usage
borrowedInto: The lower traceability tables use dense but readable row grouping for capability, contract, owner, and next-track mapping.
rejectedOrConstrained: Carbon table conventions informed layout ergonomics only and did not alter the underlying verdict law.
